revision.comment: 新規
revision.text:
'''宮山遺跡'''（みややまいせき）は[[兵庫県]][[加古川市]]にある[[縄文時代]]から[[平安時代]]までの住居跡、[[古墳]]群などで構成される遺跡。'''宮山古墳'''とも。1968年に加古川市の遺跡に指定された。
帆立貝の形状をした宮山大塚古墳を中心に、直径15m～20mの円墳5基が北に、1基が南に配置されており、東側が縄文時代の住居跡である。西側の展望広場と散策路は宮山農村公園として整備されたもの。
==主な遺跡== *敷石住居跡1棟 - 縄文時代後期 *祭祀跡 - 弥生時代 *建物跡の変遷 - 弥生時代～平安時代、 *宮山大塚古墳 - 古墳時代中期（5世紀）、前方後円墳、直径40m、高さ6m、幅6mの環濠 *1号墳 - 横穴式石室（現存）、円墳、古墳時代後期 *2号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期、直径20m *3号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *4号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *5号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *6号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期
==宮山農村公園== 国の農村総合整備モデル事業として整備された公園である。 高台に在る宮山遺跡に至る道や古墳の間を縫う道が散策路として、宮山大塚古墳の西側が広場として整備された。面積は2,655.91m2
==所在地== 〒 兵庫県加古川市八幡町上西条
==交通アクセス== *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[加古川線]] [[厄神駅]] 徒歩20分
==周辺情報== *北山公園・[[西条廃寺跡]] (国指定[[史跡]]） *[[西条古墳群]] (国指定史跡） **[[行者塚古墳]] (国指定史跡） **尼塚古墳 (国指定史跡） **人塚古墳 (国指定史跡） *城山 **西条八幡神社 **愛宕神社 *稲根神社（二塚古墳） *[[宗佐厄神八幡神社]] *[[日岡山公園]] **[[日岡御陵]] **[[日岡神社]]
{{stub}} [[Category:加古川市|みややまいせき]] [[category:兵庫県の建築物・観光名所|みややまいせき]] [[category:日本の遺跡|みややまいせき]] [[category:古墳|みややまいせき]]

'''宮山遺跡'''（みややまいせき）は[[兵庫県]][[加古川市]]にある[[縄文時代]]から[[平安時代]]までの住居跡、[[古墳]]群などで構成される[[遺跡]]。'''宮山古墳'''とも。1968年に加古川市の史跡に指定された。

==主な遺跡== *敷石住居跡1棟 - [[縄文時代]]後期 *祭祀跡 - [[弥生時代]] *建物跡の変遷 - 弥生時代～平安時代、 *宮山大塚古墳 - [[古墳時代]]中期（5世紀）、[[前方後円墳]]、直径40m、高さ6m、幅6mの環濠跡 *1号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期、直径20m *2号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *3号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *4号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *5号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *6号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期
==宮山農村公園== 国の農村総合整備モデル事業として整備された公園である。 高台に在る宮山遺跡に至る道や古墳の間を縫う道が散策路として、宮山大塚古墳の西側が広場として整備された。面積は2,655.91m2

==交通アクセス== *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[加古川線]] [[厄神駅]] 徒歩20分

{{右| [[画像:Miyayama otsuka kofun960.jpg|thumb|none|200px|宮山大塚古墳]] [[画像:Miyayama1 1024.jpg|thumb|none|150px|宮山1号墳]] [[画像:Miyayama1sekishitsu1024.jpg|thumb|none|150px|宮山1号墳の石室]] [[画像:Miyayama2 1024.jpg|thumb|none|150px|宮山2号墳]] [[画像:Miyayama3 1024a.jpg|thumb|none|150px|宮山3号墳]] [[画像:Miyayama4 1024.jpg|thumb|none|150px|宮山4号墳]] [[画像:Miyayama5 1024.jpg|thumb|none|150px|宮山5号墳]] [[画像:Miyayama6 1024.jpg|thumb|none|150px|宮山6号墳]] [[画像:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|thumb|none|150px|縄文時代の住居跡]] }} '''宮山遺跡'''（みややまいせき）は[[兵庫県]][[加古川市]]にある[[縄文時代]]から[[平安時代]]までの住居跡、[[古墳]]群などで構成される[[遺跡]]。'''宮山古墳'''とも。1968年に市史跡に指定された。
==概要== [[帆立貝]]の形状をした'''宮山大塚古墳'''を中心に、直径15m～20mの円墳5基が北に、1基が南に配置されており、東側に縄文時代の住居跡がある。 西側の展望広場と散策路は'''宮山農村公園'''として整備されたもの。
==主な遺跡== *敷石住居跡1棟 - [[縄文時代]]後期 *祭祀跡 - [[弥生時代]] *建物跡の変遷 - 弥生時代～平安時代、 *宮山大塚古墳 - [[古墳時代]]中期（5世紀）、[[前方後円墳]]、直径40m、高さ6m、幅6mの環濠跡 *1号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期、直径20m *2号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *3号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *4号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *5号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *6号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期
==宮山農村公園== 国の農村総合整備モデル事業として整備された公園である。 高台に在る宮山遺跡に至る道や古墳の間を縫う道が散策路として、宮山大塚古墳の西側が広場として整備された。面積は2,655.91m²。
==所在地== 〒675-1204 兵庫県加古川市八幡町上西条
==交通アクセス== *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[加古川線]] [[厄神駅]] 徒歩20分
==周辺情報== *成福寺 *晶岩寺 *末沢大明神 *北山公園・[[西条廃寺跡]] (国指定[[史跡]]） *[[西条古墳群]] (国指定史跡） **[[行者塚古墳]] (国指定史跡） **尼塚古墳 (国指定史跡） **人塚古墳 (国指定史跡） *城山 **西条八幡神社 **愛宕神社 *稲根神社 **二塚古墳 *[[宗佐厄神八幡神社]] *[[日岡山公園]] **[[日岡御陵]] **[[日岡神社]]
==関連項目== *[[考古学]] *[[遺跡]] *[[日本の古墳の一覧]]
{{stub}} [[Category:加古川市|みややまいせき]] [[category:兵庫県の建築物・観光名所|みややまいせき]] [[category:考古学|みややまいせき]] [[category:古墳|みややまいせき]]

==主な遺跡== *敷石住居跡1棟 - [[縄文時代]]後期 *祭祀跡 - [[弥生時代]] *建物跡の変遷 - 弥生時代～平安時代、 *宮山大塚古墳 - [[古墳時代]]中期（5世紀）、前方後円墳、後円部直径40m、高さ6m、幅6mの環濠跡 *1号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期、直径20m *2号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *3号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *4号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *5号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *6号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期
==宮山農村公園== 国の農村総合整備モデル事業として整備された公園である。 高台に在る宮山遺跡に至る道や古墳の間を縫う道が散策路として、宮山大塚古墳の西側が広場として整備された。面積は2,655.91m²。
==所在地== 〒675-1204 兵庫県加古川市八幡町上西条
==交通アクセス== *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[加古川線]] [[厄神駅]] 徒歩20分
==周辺情報== *成福寺 *晶岩寺 *末沢大明神 *[[西条廃寺跡]]（北山公園） *[[西条古墳群]] （国指定史跡） **[[行者塚古墳]] （国指定史跡） **尼塚古墳 （国指定史跡） **人塚古墳 （国指定史跡） *城山 **西条八幡神社 **愛宕神社 *稲根神社 **二塚古墳 *[[宗佐厄神八幡神社]] *[[日岡山公園]] **[[日岡御陵]] **[[日岡神社]]
==関連項目== *[[考古学]] *[[遺跡]] *[[日本の古墳一覧]]
{{stub}} [[Category:加古川市|みややまいせき]] [[category:兵庫県の建築物・観光名所|みややまいせき]] [[category:考古学|みややまいせき]] [[category:古墳|みややまいせき]]

==主な遺跡== *敷石住居跡1棟 - [[縄文時代]]後期 *祭祀跡 - [[弥生時代]] *建物跡の変遷 - 弥生時代～平安時代、 *宮山大塚古墳 - [[古墳時代]]中期（5世紀）、前方後円墳、後円部直径40m、高さ6m、幅6mの環濠跡 *1号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期、直径20m *2号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *3号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *4号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *5号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *6号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期
==宮山農村公園== 国の農村総合整備モデル事業として整備された公園である。 高台に在る宮山遺跡に至る道や古墳の間を縫う道が散策路として、宮山大塚古墳の西側が広場として整備された。面積は2,655.91m²。
==所在地== 〒675-1204 兵庫県加古川市八幡町上西条
==交通アクセス== *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[加古川線]] [[厄神駅]] 徒歩20分
==周辺情報== *成福寺 *晶岩寺 *末沢大明神 *[[西条廃寺跡]]（北山公園） *[[西条古墳群]] （国指定史跡） **[[行者塚古墳]] （国指定史跡） **尼塚古墳 （国指定史跡） **人塚古墳 （国指定史跡） *城山 **西条八幡神社 **愛宕神社 *稲根神社 **二塚古墳 *[[日岡山公園]] **[[日岡御陵]] **[[日岡神社]] *[[宗佐厄神八幡神社]]（宗佐の厄神さん）
==関連項目== *[[考古学]] *[[遺跡]] *[[日本の古墳一覧]]

==主な遺跡== *敷石住居跡1棟 - [[縄文時代]]後期 *祭祀跡 - [[弥生時代]] *建物跡の変遷 - 弥生時代～平安時代、 *宮山大塚古墳 - [[古墳時代]]中期（5世紀）、前方後円墳、後円部直径40m、高さ6m、幅6mの環濠跡 *1号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期、直径20m *2号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *3号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *4号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *5号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *6号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期
==宮山農村公園== 国の[[農村総合整備モデル事業]]として整備された公園である。 高台に在る宮山遺跡に至る道や古墳の間を縫う道が散策路として、宮山大塚古墳の西側が広場として整備された。面積は2,655.91m²。
==所在地== 〒675-1204 兵庫県加古川市八幡町上西条
==交通アクセス== *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[加古川線]] [[厄神駅]] 徒歩20分
==周辺情報== *成福寺 *晶岩寺 *末沢大明神 *[[西条廃寺跡]]（北山公園） *[[西条古墳群]] （国の[[史跡]]） **[[行者塚古墳]] **尼塚古墳 **人塚古墳 *城山 **西条八幡神社 **愛宕神社 *稲根神社 **二塚古墳 *[[日岡山公園]] **[[日岡御陵]] **[[日岡神社]] *[[宗佐厄神八幡神社]]（宗佐の厄神さん）
==関連項目== *[[考古学]] *[[遺跡]] *[[日本の古墳一覧]]
[[Category:加古川市]] [[category:兵庫県の観光地]] [[category:日本の考古遺跡]] [[category:兵庫県の古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:みややまいせき}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|19.2|N|134|53|56.9|E|region:JP|display=title}}

[[File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa), gaikan.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|宮山古墳群 概観（中央に1号墳）}}]] '''宮山遺跡'''（みややまいせき）は、[[兵庫県]][[加古川市]]八幡町上西条にある[[遺跡]]。[[縄文時代]]から[[平安時代]]のまでの住居跡、[[古墳群]]（'''宮山古墳群'''）などから構成される。加古川市指定史跡に指定されている。
==概要== [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|宮山大塚古墳}}]] [[帆立貝]]の形状をした[[前方後円墳]]'''宮山大塚古墳'''（後円部直径40m）を中心に、北に直径15m～20mの円墳が5基、南に1基が配置されており、東側に縄文時代の住居跡がある。 西側の展望広場と散策路は'''宮山農村公園'''として整備されたもの。
==主な遺跡== *敷石住居跡1棟 - [[縄文時代]]後期 *祭祀跡 - [[弥生時代]] *建物跡の変遷 - 弥生時代～平安時代、 *宮山大塚古墳 - [[古墳時代]]中期（5世紀）、前方後円墳、後円部直径40m、高さ6m、幅6mの環濠跡 *1号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期、直径20m *2号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *3号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *4号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *5号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *6号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 <gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|縄文時代の住居跡 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|1号墳 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|1号墳 石室 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|2号墳 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|3号墳 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|4号墳 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|5号墳 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|6号墳 }} </gallery>
==宮山農村公園== 国の[[農村総合整備モデル事業]]として整備された公園である。 高台に在る宮山遺跡に至る道や古墳の間を縫う道が散策路として、宮山大塚古墳の西側が広場として整備された。面積は2,655.91[[平方メートル|m<sup>2</sup>]]。
== 文化財 == === 加古川市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 宮山遺跡 - 1968年（昭和43年）4月1日指定<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html 指定・登録文化財一覧]（加古川市教育委員会）。</ref>。

revision.comment: ミス。
revision.text:
[[File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa), gaikan.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|宮山古墳群 概観（中央に1号墳）}}]] '''宮山遺跡'''（みややまいせき）は、[[兵庫県]][[加古川市]]八幡町上西条にある[[遺跡]]。[[縄文時代]]から[[平安時代]]までの住居跡、[[古墳群]]（'''宮山古墳群'''）などから構成される。加古川市指定史跡に指定されている。
==概要== [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|宮山大塚古墳}}]] [[帆立貝]]の形状をした[[前方後円墳]]'''宮山大塚古墳'''（後円部直径40m）を中心に、北に直径15m～20mの円墳が5基、南に1基が配置されており、東側に縄文時代の住居跡がある。 西側の展望広場と散策路は'''宮山農村公園'''として整備されたもの。
==主な遺跡== *敷石住居跡1棟 - [[縄文時代]]後期 *祭祀跡 - [[弥生時代]] *建物跡の変遷 - 弥生時代～平安時代、 *宮山大塚古墳 - [[古墳時代]]中期（5世紀）、前方後円墳、後円部直径40m、高さ6m、幅6mの環濠跡 *1号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期、直径20m *2号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *3号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *4号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *5号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 *6号墳 - 横穴式石室、円墳、古墳時代後期 <gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|縄文時代の住居跡 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|1号墳 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|1号墳 石室 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|2号墳 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|3号墳 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|4号墳 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|5号墳 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|6号墳 }} </gallery>
==宮山農村公園== 国の[[農村総合整備モデル事業]]として整備された公園である。 高台に在る宮山遺跡に至る道や古墳の間を縫う道が散策路として、宮山大塚古墳の西側が広場として整備された。面積は2,655.91[[平方メートル|m<sup>2</sup>]]。
== 文化財 == === 加古川市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 宮山遺跡 - 1968年（昭和43年）4月1日指定<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html 指定・登録文化財一覧]（加古川市教育委員会）。</ref>。
== 現地情報 == '''所在地''' *〒675-1204 兵庫県加古川市八幡町上西条
'''交通アクセス''' *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[加古川線]] [[厄神駅]] 徒歩20分
'''周辺''' *成福寺 *晶岩寺 *末沢大明神 *[[西条廃寺跡]]（北山公園） *[[西条古墳群]] （国の[[史跡]]） **[[行者塚古墳]] **尼塚古墳 **人塚古墳 *城山 **西条八幡神社 **愛宕神社 *稲根神社 **二塚古墳 *[[日岡山公園]] **[[日岡御陵]] **[[日岡神社]] *[[宗佐厄神八幡神社]]（宗佐の厄神さん）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)|宮山古墳群}}
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|47|19.2|N|134|53|56.9|E|region:JP|display=title}}
{{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=宮山古墳群 (加古川市) |1-1=兵庫県の古墳 |1-2=加古川市の歴史 |1-3=古墳群 |redirect2=宮山大塚古墳 |2-1=兵庫県の古墳 |2-2=加古川市の歴史 |2-3=帆立貝形古墳 }} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みややまいせき}} [[category:兵庫県の考古遺跡]] [[category:縄文時代の遺跡]] [[category:弥生時代の遺跡]] [[Category:加古川市の歴史]] [[category:兵庫県内の市町村指定史跡]]

revision.text:
[[File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa), gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the Miyayama Kofun Group (Mound 1 at the center)}}]] {{Location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture| label = Miyayama Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map.}}}}
'''Miyayama Site''' ({{ill|Miyayama Site|ja|宮山遺跡}}) is a {{ill|composite archaeological site|ja|複合遺跡}} located in Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyogo Prefecture. The site encompasses settlements from the {{ill|Jomon period|ja|縄文時代}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, as well as the {{ill|Miyayama Kofun Group|ja|宮山古墳群}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It has been designated as a cultural property by Kakogawa City.
== Overview == [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Miyayama Otsuka Kofun}}]] The central feature of the site is the {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} called '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''', with a posterior diameter of 40 meters. Surrounding it are five smaller circular tumuli (diameters 15–20 meters) to the north and one to the south. On the eastern side, remains of a Jomon-period settlement, including a {{ill|paved stone structure|ja|敷石建物}}, have been discovered. The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late {{ill|Jomon period|ja|縄文時代}} * '''Ritual Site''' – {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.
== Visitor Information == '''Address''' 675-1204, Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyogo Prefecture
'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Yakujin Station|ja|厄神駅}} on the JR Kakogawa Line
'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡}} (Kitayama Park) * {{ill|Saijō Kofun Cluster|ja|西条古墳群}} (National Historic Site) - {{ill|Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳}} - Nizuka Kofun - Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama - Saijo Hachiman Shrine - Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine - Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園}} - {{ill|Hioka Goryo|ja|日岡御陵}} - {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)|宮山古墳群}}

<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>

revision.text:
[[File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa), gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the Miyayama Kofun Group (Mound 1 at the center)}}]] {{Location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture| label = Miyayama Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map.}}}}
'''Miyayama Site''' ({{ill|Miyayama Site|ja|宮山遺跡}}) is a {{ill|composite archaeological site|ja|複合遺跡}} located in Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyogo Prefecture. The site encompasses settlements from the {{ill|Jomon period|ja|縄文時代}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, as well as the {{ill|Miyayama Kofun Group|ja|宮山古墳群}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It has been designated as a cultural property by Kakogawa City.
== Overview == [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Miyayama Otsuka Kofun}}]] The central feature of the site is the {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} called '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''', with a posterior diameter of 40 meters. Surrounding it are five smaller circular tumuli (diameters 15–20 meters) to the north and one to the south. On the eastern side, remains of a Jomon-period settlement, including a {{ill|paved stone structure|ja|敷石建物}}, have been discovered. The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late {{ill|Jomon period|ja|縄文時代}} * '''Ritual Site''' – {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.
== Visitor Information == '''Address''' 675-1204, Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyogo Prefecture
'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Yakujin Station|ja|厄神駅}} on the JR Kakogawa Line
'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡}} (Kitayama Park) * {{ill|Saijō Kofun Cluster|ja|西条古墳群}} (National Historic Site) - {{ill|Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳}} - Nizuka Kofun - Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama - Saijo Hachiman Shrine - Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine - Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園}} - {{ill|Hioka Goryo|ja|日岡御陵}} - {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)|宮山古墳群}}
{{Coord|34|47|19.2|N|134|53|56.9|E|region:JP|display=title}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa), gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the Miyayama Kofun Group (Mound 1 at the center)}}]] {{Location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture| label = Miyayama Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map.}}}}
'''Miyayama Site''' ({{ill|Miyayama Site|ja|宮山遺跡}}) is a {{ill|composite archaeological site|ja|複合遺跡}} located in Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyogo Prefecture. The site encompasses settlements from the {{ill|Jomon period|ja|縄文時代}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, as well as the {{ill|Miyayama Kofun Group|ja|宮山古墳群}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It has been designated as a cultural property by Kakogawa City.
== Overview == [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Miyayama Otsuka Kofun}}]] The central feature of the site is the {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} called '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''', with a posterior diameter of 40 meters. Surrounding it are five smaller circular tumuli (diameters 15–20 meters) to the north and one to the south. On the eastern side, remains of a Jomon-period settlement, including a {{ill|paved stone structure|ja|敷石建物}}, have been discovered. The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late {{ill|Jomon period|ja|縄文時代}} * '''Ritual Site''' – {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.
== Visitor Information == '''Address''' 675-1204, Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyogo Prefecture
'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Yakujin Station|ja|厄神駅}} on the JR Kakogawa Line
'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡}} (Kitayama Park) * {{ill|Saijō Kofun Cluster|ja|西条古墳群}} (National Historic Site) - {{ill|Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳}} - Nizuka Kofun - Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama - Saijo Hachiman Shrine - Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine - Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園}} - {{ill|Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja|日岡御陵}} - {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)|宮山古墳群}}

revision.text:
[[File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa), gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the Miyayama Kofun Group (Mound 1 at the center)}}]] {{Location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture| label = Miyayama Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map.}}}}
'''Miyayama Site''' ({{ill|Miyayama Site|ja|宮山遺跡}}) is a {{ill|composite archaeological site|ja|複合遺跡}} located in Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyogo Prefecture. The site encompasses settlements from the {{ill|Jomon period|ja|縄文時代}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, as well as the {{ill|Miyayama Kofun Group|ja|宮山古墳群}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It has been designated as a cultural property by Kakogawa City.
== Overview == [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Miyayama Otsuka Kofun}}]] The central feature of the site is the {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} called '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''', with a posterior diameter of 40 meters. Surrounding it are five smaller circular tumuli (diameters 15–20 meters) to the north and one to the south. On the eastern side, remains of a Jomon-period settlement, including a {{ill|paved stone structure|ja|敷石建物}}, have been discovered. The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late {{ill|Jomon period|ja|縄文時代}} * '''Ritual Site''' – {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.
== Visitor Information == '''Address''' 675-1204, Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyogo Prefecture
'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Yakujin Station|ja|厄神駅}} on the JR Kakogawa Line
'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡}} (Kitayama Park) * {{ill|Saijō Kofun Cluster|ja|西条古墳群}} (National Historic Site) - {{ill|Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳}} - Nizuka Kofun - Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama - Saijo Hachiman Shrine - Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine - Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園}} - {{ill|Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja|日岡御陵}} - {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)|宮山古墳群}}

revision.text:
[[File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa), gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the Miyayama Kofun Group (Mound 1 at the center)}}]] {{Location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture| label = Miyayama Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map.}}}}
'''Miyayama Site''' ({{ill|Miyayama Site|ja|宮山遺跡}}) is a {{ill|composite archaeological site|ja|複合遺跡}} located in Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyogo Prefecture. The site encompasses settlements from the {{ill|Jomon period|ja|縄文時代}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, as well as the {{ill|Miyayama Kofun Group|ja|宮山古墳群}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It has been designated as a cultural property by Kakogawa City.
== Overview == [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Miyayama Otsuka Kofun}}]] The central feature of the site is the {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} called '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''', with a posterior diameter of 40 meters. Surrounding it are five smaller circular tumuli (diameters 15–20 meters) to the north and one to the south. On the eastern side, remains of a Jomon-period settlement, including a {{ill|paved stone structure|ja|敷石建物}}, have been discovered. The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late {{ill|Jomon period|ja|縄文時代}} * '''Ritual Site''' – {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.
== Visitor Information == '''Address''' 675-1204, Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyogo Prefecture
'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Yakujin Station|ja|厄神駅}} on the JR Kakogawa Line
'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡}} (Kitayama Park) * {{ill|Saijō Kofun Cluster|ja|西条古墳群}} (National Historic Site) - {{ill|Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳}} - Nizuka Kofun - Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama - Saijo Hachiman Shrine - Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine - Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園}} - {{ill|Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja|日岡御陵}} - {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)|宮山古墳群}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11454084]] Wikidata name: Miyayama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa), gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the Miyayama Kofun Group (Mound 1 at the center)}}]] {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture| label = Miyayama Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map.}}}}
'''Miyayama Site''' ({{ill|Miyayama Site|ja|宮山遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Site|qq=}}) is a {{ill|composite archaeological site|ja|複合遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/composite archaeological site|qq=|1=multi-period archaeological site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186758}} located in Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture. The site encompasses settlements from the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], as well as the {{ill|Miyayama Kofun Group|ja|宮山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It has been designated as a cultural property by Kakogawa City.
== Overview == [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Miyayama Otsuka Kofun}}]] The central feature of the site is the {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} called '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''', with a posterior diameter of 40 meters. Surrounding it are five smaller circular tumuli (diameters 15–20 meters) to the north and one to the south. On the eastern side, remains of a Jomon-period settlement, including a {{ill|E-kagami-style pit dwelling |lt=paved stone structure|ja|敷石建物|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paved stone structure|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柄鏡形竪穴建物}}, have been discovered. The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] * '''Ritual Site''' – [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|qq=|1=Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637961}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.
== Visitor Information == '''Address''' 675-1204, Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture
'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from [[:en:Yakujin Station|Yakujin Station]] on the JR Kakogawa Line
'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijo Temple Ruins|qq=}} (Kitayama Park) * [[:en:Saijō Kofun Cluster|Saijō Kofun Cluster]] (National Historic Site) ** {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyosha-zuka Kofun|qq=}} ** Nizuka Kofun ** Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama ** Saijo Hachiman Shrine ** Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine ** Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokayama Park|qq=|1=Hiokayama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505276}} ** {{ill|Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja|日岡御陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Goryo Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日岡陵古墳}} ** {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Shrine|qq=|1=Hioka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505275}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|qq=|1=Sousayakujin-hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451841}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11454084]] Wikidata name: Miyayama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa), gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the Miyayama Kofun Group (Mound 1 at the center)}}]] {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture| label = Miyayama Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map.}}}}
'''Miyayama Site''' ({{ill|Miyayama Site|ja|宮山遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Site|qq=}}) is a {{ill|composite archaeological site|ja|複合遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/composite archaeological site|1=multi-period archaeological site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186758}} located in Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture. The site encompasses settlements from the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], as well as the {{ill|Miyayama Kofun Group|ja|宮山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It has been designated as a cultural property by Kakogawa City.
== Overview == [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Miyayama Otsuka Kofun}}]] The central feature of the site is the {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} called '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''', with a posterior diameter of 40 meters. Surrounding it are five smaller circular tumuli (diameters 15–20 meters) to the north and one to the south. On the eastern side, remains of a Jomon-period settlement, including a {{ill|E-kagami-style pit dwelling |lt=paved stone structure|ja|敷石建物|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paved stone structure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柄鏡形竪穴建物}}, have been discovered. The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] * '''Ritual Site''' – [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|1=Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637961}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.
== Visitor Information == '''Address''' 675-1204, Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture
'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from [[:en:Yakujin Station|Yakujin Station]] on the JR Kakogawa Line
'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijo Temple Ruins|qq=}} (Kitayama Park) * [[:en:Saijō Kofun Cluster|Saijō Kofun Cluster]] (National Historic Site) ** {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyosha-zuka Kofun|qq=}} ** Nizuka Kofun ** Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama ** Saijo Hachiman Shrine ** Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine ** Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokayama Park|1=Hiokayama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505276}} ** {{ill|Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja|日岡御陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日岡陵古墳}} ** {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Shrine|1=Hioka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505275}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|1=Sousayakujin-hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451841}}

<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|1=Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637961}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.

'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from [[:en:Yakujin Station|Yakujin Station]] on the JR Kakogawa Line

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11454084]] Wikidata name: Miyayama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa), gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the Miyayama Kofun Group (Mound 1 at the center)}}]] {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture| label = Miyayama Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map.}}}}
'''Miyayama Site''' ({{ill|Miyayama Site|ja|宮山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Site|qq=}}) is a {{ill|composite archaeological site|ja|複合遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/composite archaeological site|1=multi-period archaeological site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186758}} located in Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture. The site encompasses settlements from the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], as well as the {{ill|Miyayama Kofun Group|ja|宮山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It has been designated as a cultural property by Kakogawa City.
== Overview == [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Miyayama Otsuka Kofun}}]] The central feature of the site is the {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} called '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''', with a posterior diameter of 40 meters. Surrounding it are five smaller circular tumuli (diameters 15–20 meters) to the north and one to the south. On the eastern side, remains of a Jomon-period settlement, including a {{ill|E-kagami-style pit dwelling |lt=paved stone structure|ja|敷石建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paved stone structure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柄鏡形竪穴建物}}, have been discovered. The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] * '''Ritual Site''' – [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|1=Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637961}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.

'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijo Temple Ruins|qq=}} (Kitayama Park) * [[:en:Saijō Kofun Cluster|Saijō Kofun Cluster]] (National Historic Site) ** {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyosha-zuka Kofun|qq=}} ** Nizuka Kofun ** Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama ** Saijo Hachiman Shrine ** Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine ** Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokayama Park|1=Hiokayama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505276}} ** {{ill|Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja|日岡御陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日岡陵古墳}} ** {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Shrine|1=Hioka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505275}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|1=Sousayakujin-hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451841}}

'''Miyayama Site''' ({{ill|Miyayama Site|ja|宮山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Site|qq=}}) is a {{ill|composite archaeological site|ja|複合遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/composite archaeological site|1=multi-period archaeological site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186758}} located in Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture. The site encompasses settlements from the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], as well as the {{ill|Miyayama Kofun Group|ja|宮山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It has been designated as a cultural property by Kakogawa City.
== Overview == [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Miyayama Otsuka Kofun}}]] The central feature of the site is the {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} called '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''', with a posterior diameter of 40 meters. Surrounding it are five smaller circular tumuli (diameters 15–20 meters) to the north and one to the south. On the eastern side, remains of a Jomon-period settlement, including a {{ill|E-kagami-style pit dwelling |lt=paved stone structure|ja|敷石建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paved stone structure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柄鏡形竪穴建物}}, have been discovered. The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] * '''Ritual Site''' – [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|1=Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637961}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.
== Visitor Information == '''Address''' 675-1204, Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture
'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from [[:en:Yakujin Station|Yakujin Station]] on the JR Kakogawa Line
'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijo Temple Ruins|qq=}} (Kitayama Park) * [[:en:Saijō Kofun Cluster|Saijō Kofun Cluster]] (National Historic Site) ** {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyosha-zuka Kofun|qq=}} ** Nizuka Kofun ** Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama ** Saijo Hachiman Shrine ** Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine ** Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokayama Park|1=Hiokayama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505276}} ** {{ill|Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja|日岡御陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日岡陵古墳}} ** {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Shrine|1=Hioka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505275}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|1=Sousayakujin-hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451841}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11454084]] Wikidata name: Miyayama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa), gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the Miyayama Kofun Group (Mound 1 at the center)}}]] {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture| label = Miyayama Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map.}}}}
'''Miyayama Site''' ({{ill|Miyayama Site|ja|宮山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Site|qq=}}) is a {{ill|composite archaeological site|ja|複合遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/composite archaeological site|1=multi-period archaeological site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186758}} located in Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture. The site encompasses settlements from the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], as well as the {{ill|Miyayama Kofun Group|ja|宮山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It has been designated as a cultural property by Kakogawa City.
== Overview == [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Miyayama Otsuka Kofun}}]] The central feature of the site is the {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} called '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''', with a posterior diameter of 40 meters. Surrounding it are five smaller circular tumuli (diameters 15–20 meters) to the north and one to the south. On the eastern side, remains of a Jomon-period settlement, including a {{ill|E-kagami-style pit dwelling |lt=paved stone structure|ja|敷石建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paved stone structure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柄鏡形竪穴建物}}, have been discovered. The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] * '''Ritual Site''' – [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|1=Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637961}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.
== Visitor Information == '''Address''' 675-1204, Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture
'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from [[:en:Yakujin Station|Yakujin Station]] on the JR Kakogawa Line
'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijo Temple Ruins|qq=}} (Kitayama Park) * [[:en:Saijō Kofun Cluster|Saijō Kofun Cluster]] (National Historic Site) ** {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyosha-zuka Kofun|qq=}} ** Nizuka Kofun ** Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama ** Saijo Hachiman Shrine ** Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine ** Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokayama Park|1=Hiokayama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505276}} ** {{ill|Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja|日岡御陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日岡陵古墳}} ** {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Shrine|1=Hioka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505275}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|1=Sousayakujin-hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451841}}

== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] * '''Ritual Site''' – [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|1=Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637961}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.
== Visitor Information == '''Address''' 675-1204, Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture
'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from [[:en:Yakujin Station|Yakujin Station]] on the JR Kakogawa Line
'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijo Temple Ruins|qq=}} (Kitayama Park) * [[:en:Saijō Kofun Cluster|Saijō Kofun Cluster]] (National Historic Site) ** {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyosha-zuka Kofun|qq=}} ** Nizuka Kofun ** Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama ** Saijo Hachiman Shrine ** Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine ** Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokayama Park|1=Hiokayama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505276}} ** {{ill|Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja|日岡御陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日岡陵古墳}} ** {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Shrine|1=Hioka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505275}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|1=Sousayakujin-hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451841}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11454084]] Wikidata name: Miyayama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa), gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the Miyayama Kofun Group (Mound 1 at the center)}}]] {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture| label = Miyayama Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map.}}}}
'''Miyayama Site''' ({{ill|Miyayama Site|ja|宮山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Site|qq=}}) is a {{ill|composite archaeological site|ja|複合遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/composite archaeological site|1=multi-period archaeological site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186758}} located in Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture. The site encompasses settlements from the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], as well as the {{ill|Miyayama Kofun Group|ja|宮山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It has been designated as a cultural property by Kakogawa City.
== Overview == [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Miyayama Otsuka Kofun}}]] The central feature of the site is the {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} called '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''', with a posterior diameter of 40 meters. Surrounding it are five smaller circular tumuli (diameters 15–20 meters) to the north and one to the south. On the eastern side, remains of a Jomon-period settlement, including a {{ill|E-kagami-style pit dwelling |lt=paved stone structure|ja|敷石建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paved stone structure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柄鏡形竪穴建物}}, have been discovered. The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] * '''Ritual Site''' – [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|1=Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637961}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.
== Visitor Information == '''Address''' 675-1204, Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture
'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from [[:en:Yakujin Station|Yakujin Station]] on the JR Kakogawa Line
'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijo Temple Ruins|qq=}} (Kitayama Park) * [[:en:Saijō Kofun Cluster|Saijō Kofun Cluster]] (National Historic Site) ** {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyosha-zuka Kofun|qq=}} ** Nizuka Kofun ** Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama ** Saijo Hachiman Shrine ** Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine ** Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokayama Park|1=Hiokayama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505276}} ** {{ill|Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja|日岡御陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日岡陵古墳}} ** {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Shrine|1=Hioka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505275}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|1=Sousayakujin-hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451841}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11454084]] Wikidata name: Miyayama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa), gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the Miyayama Kofun Group (Mound 1 at the center)}}]] {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture| label = Miyayama Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map.}}}}
'''Miyayama Site''' ({{ill|Miyayama Site|ja|宮山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Site|qq=}}) is a {{ill|composite archaeological site|ja|複合遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/composite archaeological site|1=multi-period archaeological site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186758}} located in Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture. The site encompasses settlements from the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], as well as the {{ill|Miyayama Kofun Group|ja|宮山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It has been designated as a cultural property by Kakogawa City.
== Overview == [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Miyayama Otsuka Kofun}}]] The central feature of the site is the {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} called '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''', with a posterior diameter of 40 meters. Surrounding it are five smaller circular tumuli (diameters 15–20 meters) to the north and one to the south. On the eastern side, remains of a Jomon-period settlement, including a {{ill|E-kagami-style pit dwelling |lt=paved stone structure|ja|敷石建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paved stone structure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柄鏡形竪穴建物}}, have been discovered. The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] * '''Ritual Site''' – [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|1=Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637961}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.
== Visitor Information == '''Address''' 675-1204, Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture
'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from [[:en:Yakujin Station|Yakujin Station]] on the JR Kakogawa Line
'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijo Temple Ruins|qq=}} (Kitayama Park) * [[:en:Saijō Kofun Cluster|Saijō Kofun Cluster]] (National Historic Site) ** {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyosha-zuka Kofun|qq=}} ** Nizuka Kofun ** Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama ** Saijo Hachiman Shrine ** Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine ** Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokayama Park|1=Hiokayama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505276}} ** {{ill|Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja|日岡御陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日岡陵古墳}} ** {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Shrine|1=Hioka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505275}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|1=Sousayakujin-hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451841}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)|宮山古墳群}}
