revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11454084]] Wikidata name: Miyayama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa), gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the Miyayama Kofun Group (Mound 1 at the center)}}]] {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture| label = Miyayama Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map.}}}}
'''Miyayama Site''' ({{ill|Miyayama Site|ja|宮山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Site|qq=}}) is a {{ill|composite archaeological site|ja|複合遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/composite archaeological site|1=multi-period archaeological site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186758}} located in Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture. The site encompasses settlements from the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], as well as the {{ill|Miyayama Kofun Group|ja|宮山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It has been designated as a cultural property by Kakogawa City.
== Overview == [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Miyayama Otsuka Kofun}}]] The central feature of the site is the {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} called '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''', with a posterior diameter of 40 meters. Surrounding it are five smaller circular tumuli (diameters 15–20 meters) to the north and one to the south. On the eastern side, remains of a Jomon-period settlement, including a {{ill|E-kagami-style pit dwelling |lt=paved stone structure|ja|敷石建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paved stone structure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柄鏡形竪穴建物}}, have been discovered. The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] * '''Ritual Site''' – [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|1=Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637961}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.
== Visitor Information == '''Address''' 675-1204, Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture
'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from [[:en:Yakujin Station|Yakujin Station]] on the JR Kakogawa Line
'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijo Temple Ruins|qq=}} (Kitayama Park) * [[:en:Saijō Kofun Cluster|Saijō Kofun Cluster]] (National Historic Site) ** {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyosha-zuka Kofun|qq=}} ** Nizuka Kofun ** Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama ** Saijo Hachiman Shrine ** Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine ** Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokayama Park|1=Hiokayama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505276}} ** {{ill|Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja|日岡御陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日岡陵古墳}} ** {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Shrine|1=Hioka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505275}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|1=Sousayakujin-hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451841}}

== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] * '''Ritual Site''' – [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|1=Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637961}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.
== Visitor Information == '''Address''' 675-1204, Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture
'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from [[:en:Yakujin Station|Yakujin Station]] on the JR Kakogawa Line
'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijo Temple Ruins|qq=}} (Kitayama Park) * [[:en:Saijō Kofun Cluster|Saijō Kofun Cluster]] (National Historic Site) ** {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyosha-zuka Kofun|qq=}} ** Nizuka Kofun ** Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama ** Saijo Hachiman Shrine ** Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine ** Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokayama Park|1=Hiokayama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505276}} ** {{ill|Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja|日岡御陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日岡陵古墳}} ** {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Shrine|1=Hioka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505275}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|1=Sousayakujin-hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451841}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11454084]] Wikidata name: Miyayama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa), gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the Miyayama Kofun Group (Mound 1 at the center)}}]] {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture| label = Miyayama Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map.}}}}
'''Miyayama Site''' ({{ill|Miyayama Site|ja|宮山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Site|qq=}}) is a {{ill|composite archaeological site|ja|複合遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/composite archaeological site|1=multi-period archaeological site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186758}} located in Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture. The site encompasses settlements from the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], as well as the {{ill|Miyayama Kofun Group|ja|宮山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It has been designated as a cultural property by Kakogawa City.
== Overview == [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Miyayama Otsuka Kofun}}]] The central feature of the site is the {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} called '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''', with a posterior diameter of 40 meters. Surrounding it are five smaller circular tumuli (diameters 15–20 meters) to the north and one to the south. On the eastern side, remains of a Jomon-period settlement, including a {{ill|E-kagami-style pit dwelling |lt=paved stone structure|ja|敷石建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paved stone structure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柄鏡形竪穴建物}}, have been discovered. The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] * '''Ritual Site''' – [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|1=Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637961}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.

'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijo Temple Ruins|qq=}} (Kitayama Park) * [[:en:Saijō Kofun Cluster|Saijō Kofun Cluster]] (National Historic Site) ** {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyosha-zuka Kofun|qq=}} ** Nizuka Kofun ** Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama ** Saijo Hachiman Shrine ** Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine ** Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokayama Park|1=Hiokayama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505276}} ** {{ill|Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja|日岡御陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日岡陵古墳}} ** {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Shrine|1=Hioka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505275}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|1=Sousayakujin-hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451841}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11454084]] Wikidata name: Miyayama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa), gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the Miyayama Kofun Group (Mound 1 at the center)}}]] {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture| label = Miyayama Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map.}}}}
'''Miyayama Site''' ({{ill|Miyayama Site|ja|宮山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Site|qq=}}) is a {{ill|composite archaeological site|ja|複合遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/composite archaeological site|1=multi-period archaeological site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186758}} located in Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture. The site encompasses settlements from the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], as well as the {{ill|Miyayama Kofun Group|ja|宮山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It has been designated as a cultural property by Kakogawa City.
== Overview == [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Miyayama Otsuka Kofun}}]] The central feature of the site is the {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} called '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''', with a posterior diameter of 40 meters. Surrounding it are five smaller circular tumuli (diameters 15–20 meters) to the north and one to the south. On the eastern side, remains of a Jomon-period settlement, including a {{ill|E-kagami-style pit dwelling |lt=paved stone structure|ja|敷石建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paved stone structure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柄鏡形竪穴建物}}, have been discovered. The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] * '''Ritual Site''' – [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|1=Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637961}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.
== Visitor Information == '''Address''' 675-1204, Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture
'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from [[:en:Yakujin Station|Yakujin Station]] on the JR Kakogawa Line
'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijo Temple Ruins|qq=}} (Kitayama Park) * [[:en:Saijō Kofun Cluster|Saijō Kofun Cluster]] (National Historic Site) ** {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyosha-zuka Kofun|qq=}} ** Nizuka Kofun ** Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama ** Saijo Hachiman Shrine ** Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine ** Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokayama Park|1=Hiokayama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505276}} ** {{ill|Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja|日岡御陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日岡陵古墳}} ** {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Shrine|1=Hioka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505275}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|1=Sousayakujin-hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451841}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)|宮山古墳群}}

Specialized pedestal stands and jars The Miyayama Tokushu Kidai has only been found at the {{ill|Miyayama Site|ja|宮山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Site|qq=}} in Sōja. The rim is thick and inward-slanting, with unique structural fea ... 14 KB (1,850 words) - 21:42, 15 January

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11454084]] Wikidata name: Miyayama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa), gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the Miyayama Kofun Group (Mound 1 at the center)}}]] {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture| label = Miyayama Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map.}}}}
'''Miyayama Site''' ({{ill|Miyayama Site|ja|宮山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Site|qq=}}) is a {{ill|composite archaeological site|ja|複合遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/composite archaeological site|1=multi-period archaeological site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186758}} located in Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture. The site encompasses settlements from the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], as well as the {{ill|Miyayama Kofun Group|ja|宮山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It has been designated as a cultural property by Kakogawa City.
== Overview == [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Miyayama Otsuka Kofun}}]] The central feature of the site is the {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} called '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''', with a posterior diameter of 40 meters. Surrounding it are five smaller circular tumuli (diameters 15–20 meters) to the north and one to the south. On the eastern side, remains of a Jomon-period settlement, including a {{ill|E-kagami-style pit dwelling |lt=paved stone structure|ja|敷石建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paved stone structure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柄鏡形竪穴建物}}, have been discovered. The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]] * '''Ritual Site''' – [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|1=Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637961}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.
== Visitor Information == '''Address''' 675-1204, Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture
'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from [[:en:Yakujin Station|Yakujin Station]] on the JR Kakogawa Line
'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijo Temple Ruins|qq=}} (Kitayama Park) * [[:en:Saijō Kofun Cluster|Saijō Kofun Cluster]] (National Historic Site) ** {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyosha-zuka Kofun|qq=}} ** Nizuka Kofun ** Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama ** Saijo Hachiman Shrine ** Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine ** Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokayama Park|1=Hiokayama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505276}} ** {{ill|Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja|日岡御陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日岡陵古墳}} ** {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Shrine|1=Hioka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505275}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|1=Sousayakujin-hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451841}}

The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jomon period}} * '''Ritual Site''' – {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}, with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|1=Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637961}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.

'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Yakujin Station|en|Yakujin Station|WD=Q1000516|lt=Yakujin Station}} on the JR Kakogawa Line

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11454084]] Wikidata name: Miyayama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa), gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the Miyayama Kofun Group (Mound 1 at the center)}}]] {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture| label = Miyayama Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map.}}}}
'''Miyayama Site''' ({{ill|Miyayama Site|ja|宮山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Site|qq=}}) is a {{ill|composite archaeological site|ja|複合遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/composite archaeological site|1=multi-period archaeological site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186758}} located in Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture. The site encompasses settlements from the {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jomon period}} to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, as well as the {{ill|Miyayama Kofun Group|ja|宮山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. It has been designated as a cultural property by Kakogawa City.
== Overview == [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Miyayama Otsuka Kofun}}]] The central feature of the site is the {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} called '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''', with a posterior diameter of 40 meters. Surrounding it are five smaller circular tumuli (diameters 15–20 meters) to the north and one to the south. On the eastern side, remains of a Jomon-period settlement, including a {{ill|E-kagami-style pit dwelling |lt=paved stone structure|ja|敷石建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paved stone structure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柄鏡形竪穴建物}}, have been discovered. The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jomon period}} * '''Ritual Site''' – {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}, with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|1=Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637961}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.

'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Yakujin Station|en|Yakujin Station|WD=Q1000516|lt=Yakujin Station}} on the JR Kakogawa Line
'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijo Temple Ruins|qq=}} (Kitayama Park) * {{ill|Saijō Kofun Cluster|en|Saijō Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11628707|lt=Saijō Kofun Cluster}} (National Historic Site) ** {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyosha-zuka Kofun|qq=}} ** Nizuka Kofun ** Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama ** Saijo Hachiman Shrine ** Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine ** Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokayama Park|1=Hiokayama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505276}} ** {{ill|Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja|日岡御陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日岡陵古墳}} ** {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Shrine|1=Hioka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505275}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|1=Sousayakujin-hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451841}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]]
[[File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa), gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the Miyayama Kofun Group (Mound 1 at the center)}}]] {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture| label = Miyayama Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map.}}}}
'''Miyayama Site''' ({{ill|Miyayama Site|ja|宮山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Site|qq=}}) is a {{ill|composite archaeological site|ja|複合遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/composite archaeological site|1=multi-period archaeological site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186758}} located in Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture. The site encompasses settlements from the {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jomon period}} to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, as well as the {{ill|Miyayama Kofun Group|ja|宮山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. It has been designated as a cultural property by Kakogawa City.
== Overview == [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Miyayama Otsuka Kofun}}]] The central feature of the site is the {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} called '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''', with a posterior diameter of 40 meters. Surrounding it are five smaller circular tumuli (diameters 15–20 meters) to the north and one to the south. On the eastern side, remains of a Jomon-period settlement, including a {{ill|E-kagami-style pit dwelling |lt=paved stone structure|ja|敷石建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paved stone structure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柄鏡形竪穴建物}}, have been discovered. The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jomon period}} * '''Ritual Site''' – {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}, with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|1=Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637961}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.
== Visitor Information == '''Address''' 675-1204, Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture
'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Yakujin Station|en|Yakujin Station|WD=Q1000516|lt=Yakujin Station}} on the JR Kakogawa Line
'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijo Temple Ruins|qq=}} (Kitayama Park) * {{ill|Saijō Kofun Cluster|en|Saijō Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11628707|lt=Saijō Kofun Cluster}} (National Historic Site) ** {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyosha-zuka Kofun|qq=}} ** Nizuka Kofun ** Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama ** Saijo Hachiman Shrine ** Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine ** Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokayama Park|1=Hiokayama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505276}} ** {{ill|Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja|日岡御陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日岡陵古墳}} ** {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Shrine|1=Hioka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505275}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|1=Sousayakujin-hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451841}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)|宮山古墳群}}

It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.
== Visitor Information == '''Address''' 675-1204, Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture
'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Yakujin Station|en|Yakujin Station|WD=Q1000516|lt=Yakujin Station|1=Yakujin Station}} on the JR Kakogawa Line
'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijo Temple Ruins|qq=}} (Kitayama Park) * {{ill|Saijō Kofun Cluster|en|Saijō Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11628707|lt=Saijō Kofun Cluster|1=Saijō Kofun Cluster}} (National Historic Site) ** {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyosha-zuka Kofun|qq=}} ** Nizuka Kofun ** Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama ** Saijo Hachiman Shrine ** Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine ** Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokayama Park|1=Q11505276|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505276}} ** {{ill|Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja|日岡御陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日岡陵古墳}} ** {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Shrine|1=Hioka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505275}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|1=Sousayakujin-hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451841}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]]
[[File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa), gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the Miyayama Kofun Group (Mound 1 at the center)}}]] {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture| label = Miyayama Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map.}}}}
'''Miyayama Site''' ({{ill|Miyayama Site|ja|宮山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Site|qq=}}) is a {{ill|composite archaeological site|ja|複合遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/composite archaeological site|1=Complex archaeological sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97186758}} located in Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture. The site encompasses settlements from the {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jomon period|1=Jōmon period}} to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, as well as the {{ill|Miyayama Kofun Group|ja|宮山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. It has been designated as a cultural property by Kakogawa City.
== Overview == [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Miyayama Otsuka Kofun}}]] The central feature of the site is the {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} called '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''', with a posterior diameter of 40 meters. Surrounding it are five smaller circular tumuli (diameters 15–20 meters) to the north and one to the south. On the eastern side, remains of a Jomon-period settlement, including a {{ill|E-kagami-style pit dwelling |lt=paved stone structure|ja|敷石建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paved stone structure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柄鏡形竪穴建物}}, have been discovered. The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jomon period|1=Jōmon period}} * '''Ritual Site''' – {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|1=Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637961}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.
== Visitor Information == '''Address''' 675-1204, Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture
'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Yakujin Station|en|Yakujin Station|WD=Q1000516|lt=Yakujin Station|1=Yakujin Station}} on the JR Kakogawa Line
'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijo Temple Ruins|qq=}} (Kitayama Park) * {{ill|Saijō Kofun Cluster|en|Saijō Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11628707|lt=Saijō Kofun Cluster|1=Saijō Kofun Cluster}} (National Historic Site) ** {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyosha-zuka Kofun|qq=}} ** Nizuka Kofun ** Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama ** Saijo Hachiman Shrine ** Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine ** Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokayama Park|1=Q11505276|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505276}} ** {{ill|Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja|日岡御陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日岡陵古墳}} ** {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Shrine|1=Hioka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505275}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|1=Sousayakujin-hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451841}}

Surrounding it are five smaller circular tumuli (diameters 15–20 meters) to the north and one to the south. On the eastern side, remains of a Jomon-period settlement, including a {{ill|E-kagami-style pit dwelling |lt=paved stone structure|ja|敷石建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paved stone structure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柄鏡形竪穴建物}}, have been discovered. The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jomon period|1=Jōmon period}} * '''Ritual Site''' – {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|1=Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11637961}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.

'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Yakujin Station|en|Yakujin Station|WD=Q1000516|lt=Yakujin Station|1=Yakujin Station}} on the JR Kakogawa Line

The site encompasses settlements from the {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jomon period|1=Jōmon period}} to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, as well as the {{ill|Miyayama Kofun Group|ja|宮山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. It has been designated as a cultural property by Kakogawa City.
== Overview == [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Miyayama Otsuka Kofun}}]] The central feature of the site is the {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} called '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''', with a posterior diameter of 40 meters. The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|WD=Q52824|lt=Jomon period|1=Jōmon period}} * '''Ritual Site''' – {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)]][[ja:宮山遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宮山遺跡]]
[[File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa), gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Overview of the Miyayama Kofun Group (Mound 1 at the center)}}]] {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture| label = Miyayama Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Left|Location map.}}}}
'''Miyayama Site''' ({{ill|Miyayama Site|ja|宮山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Site|qq=}}) is a {{ill|composite archaeological site|ja|複合遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/composite archaeological site|1=Complex archaeological sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97186758}} located in Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture. The site encompasses settlements from the {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|qid=Q52824|lt=Jomon period|1=Jōmon period}} to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, as well as the {{ill|Miyayama Kofun Group|ja|宮山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. It has been designated as a cultural property by Kakogawa City.
== Overview == [[File:Miyayama Otsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Miyayama Otsuka Kofun}}]] The central feature of the site is the {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} called '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''', with a posterior diameter of 40 meters. Surrounding it are five smaller circular tumuli (diameters 15–20 meters) to the north and one to the south. On the eastern side, remains of a Jomon-period settlement, including a {{ill|E-kagami-style pit dwelling |lt=paved stone structure|ja|敷石建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paved stone structure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柄鏡形竪穴建物}}, have been discovered. The western side has been developed as the '''Miyayama Rural Park''', with observation areas and walking trails.
== Major Features == * '''Paved Stone Building''' – Late {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|qid=Q52824|lt=Jomon period|1=Jōmon period}} * '''Ritual Site''' – {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} * '''Settlement Transition''' – From the Yayoi to the Heian period * '''Miyayama Otsuka Kofun''' – Middle Kofun period (5th century), {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, with a posterior diameter of 40 meters and height of 6 meters, and a 6-meter-wide surrounding moat. * '''Mound 1''' – Circular tumulus with a {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (stone chamber), diameter of 20 meters, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 2''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 3''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 4''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 5''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period * '''Mound 6''' – Circular tumulus with a stone chamber, Late Kofun period
<gallery> File:Miyayama jyomonjukyo1024.jpg|Remains of a Jomon-period structure File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, funkyu.jpg|Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-1, sekishitsu.jpg|Stone chamber of Mound 1 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-2, funkyu.jpg|Mound 2 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-3, funkyu.jpg|Mound 3 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-4, funkyu.jpg|Mound 4 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-5, funkyu.jpg|Mound 5 File:Miyayama Kofun Group (Kakogawa)-6, funkyu.jpg|Mound 6 </gallery>
== Miyayama Rural Park == This park was developed under the national {{ill|Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|ja|農村総合整備モデル事業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|1=Comprehensive Rural Development Model Project|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11637961}}. It features walking paths weaving between the mounds and a plaza area west of Miyayama Otsuka Kofun. The total area is 2,655.91 square meters.
== Cultural Properties == === Kakogawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ''' Miyayama Site – Designated on April 1, 1968<ref>[https://www.city.kakogawa.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyouiku/kakuka/kyoikushidobu/bunkazai_cyosa/bunkazai/1489194630431.html List of Designated Cultural Properties] (Kakogawa City Board of Education).</ref>.
== Visitor Information == '''Address''' 675-1204, Kamisaijo, Yahata-cho, Kakogawa City, Hyōgo Prefecture
'''Access''' * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Yakujin Station|en|Yakujin Station|qid=Q1000516|lt=Yakujin Station|1=Yakujin Station}} on the JR Kakogawa Line
'''Nearby Attractions''' * Jofuku-ji Temple * Shojo-in Temple * Suezawa Daimyojin Shrine * {{ill|Saijo Temple Ruins|ja|西条廃寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijo Temple Ruins|qq=}} (Kitayama Park) * {{ill|Saijō Kofun Cluster|en|Saijō Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11628707|lt=Saijō Kofun Cluster|1=Saijō Kofun Cluster}} (National Historic Site) ** {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyōjazuka Kofun|lt=Gyosha-zuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyosha-zuka Kofun|qq=}} ** Nizuka Kofun ** Hitoduka Kofun * Shiroyama ** Saijo Hachiman Shrine ** Atago Shrine * Inane Shrine ** Futazuka Kofun * {{ill|Hiokayama Park|ja|日岡山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokayama Park|1=Q11505276|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11505276}} ** {{ill|Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja|日岡御陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Goryo Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日岡陵古墳}} ** {{ill|Hioka Shrine|ja|日岡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hioka Shrine|1=Hioka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11505275}} * {{ill|Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|ja|宗佐厄神八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sosa Yakujin Hachiman Shrine|1=Sousayakujin-hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11451841}}
